Key differences:
1. Target has a completely empty row 2 (all squares). Neither distractor has this.
2. Target has row 3 with 4 Xs (missing the last one). Distractor 1 has 5 Xs in row 3, Distractor 2 has 1 X in row 3.
3. Target has the leftmost column all filled except row 2. Distractor 1 has leftmost column all filled. Distractor 2 has leftmost column all filled.


4. Looking at the column patterns, the target shows a decreasing number of Xs as I move rightward: column 1 has 4 Xs, columns 2-4 each have 2 Xs, and column 5 has only 1 X. I should focus on the most distinctive feature—the completely empty second row sets this apart from both distractors, which have filled cells throughout. The alternating pattern in Distractor 1 and the hollow rectangle in Distractor 2 both lack this characteristic empty row.
